scenario: A support agent's Tasklist shows 30 pending User Tasks. The agent wants to prioritise -- handle the urgent ones first. The User Tasks have a priority variable (HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW) and a dueDate attribute.
question: How does Tasklist support task prioritisation by the assignee?

options.text: While Tasklist supports priority-based sorting, the default sort order may vary based on configuration and version. Priority sorting is available and configurable, but it is not guaranteed to be the automatic default ordering for every deployment. Users and administrators can configure sorting preferences, but the assumption that priority ordering is always automatic overstates the out-of-box behaviour.

Task priority sorting and filtering have no implementation in Tasklist -- all tasks would be displayed in an unordered list with no ability to sort by urgency or filter by priority criteria -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Sorting and filtering are standard features of Tasklist's user interface. Tasks can be sorted by multiple criteria. Priority-relevant metadata like due dates and follow-up dates are displayed and can be used as sort keys. Variable-based filtering enables users to surface high-priority tasks based on custom business data. These are core features of the Tasklist component.

Operate is an operational monitoring tool for process instance lifecycle management: viewing running instances, managing incidents, modifying instances, and monitoring overall process health. Tasklist is the dedicated human task management interface where users see their assigned and available tasks, complete tasks with form data, and configure task filtering and sorting preferences.

Tasklist's task list view supports sorting and filtering -- sort by priority using custom process variables or built-in sort keys like dueDate and creation date. Filter by task status, assignee, candidate group, process definition, and due-date ranges. The Tasklist V2 REST API supports the same filtering for programmatic task queue management. Operators can combine multiple filter criteria simultaneously to build targeted task views for specific team queues. Users can configure personal filter preferences to focus on their most relevant tasks. assignee picks tasks in priority order from the sorted list.

a.text: Incorrect. Tasklist's primary value is helping assignees manage their work. Sorting / filtering options include: by dueDate (earliest first / latest first), by creation date, by priority (if your modelling sets a priority variable or uses the built-in priority concept), by candidate group, by other filters. The exact UI affordances vary by Tasklist version; verify current docs. Modelling tip: explicitly set a priority variable on each User Task (via Input Mapping with a FEEL expression), then Tasklist filtering / sorting works on that. Or use the User Task's built-in priority attribute (where supported).

b.text: Incorrect. Auto-ordering depends on configuration; user can typically override.
